title: Election Day (politics)

infobox.value: This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Election_day "Special:EditPage/Election day") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Election day"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Election+day%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Election+day%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Election+day%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Election+day%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Election+day%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Election+day%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))*
Region | Election day | political system
Europe | Sunday.[[3]](#cite_note-3) | Parliamentary republic
South America | Fourth Sunday of October immediately before the end of the current mandates.[[4]](#cite_note-4) | federal presidential republic

Europe | Sunday. [Nationalrat](/wiki/National_Council_(Austria) "National Council (Austria)") elections may be held on another public holiday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary republic

Oceania | Saturday for federal,[[6]](#cite_note-Australia-6) state and most local elections. Postal and early voting permitted. Some local elections are by postal voting only. | Parliamentary monarchy
Europe | Sunday (until 1894, Tuesday).[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary monarchy
South America | Sunday.[[7]](#cite_note-7) | Presidential republic
Europe | Sunday.[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTESchakel201742-8) | Parliamentary republic
South America | First Sunday of October. Runoffs take place on the last Sunday of the same October.[[9]](#cite_note-9) | Federal presidential republic
Europe | Sunday. | Parliamentary republic

North America | Third Monday of October every four years, or after Parliament is dissolved by the [Governor General](/wiki/Governor_General_of_Canada "Governor General of Canada").[[10]](#cite_note-ElectionsCanada-10) | Parliamentary monarchy
South America | Sunday.[[11]](#cite_note-11) | Presidential republic

South America | Sunday: the second Sunday of March for Congress, and the second Sunday of May for President and Vice President.[[12]](#cite_note-12) [*[check quotation syntax](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style#Punctuation "Wikipedia:Manual of Style")*]|Presidential republic

Europe | Traditionally elections are held over two days, starting on a Friday afternoon and ending the following Saturday afternoon.[[13]](#cite_note-Czech-13)[[14]](#cite_note-Oireachtas-14) | Parliamentary republic
Europe | Saturday.[[15]](#cite_note-Vaalit-15)[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Parliamentary republic

North America | Sunday: first Sunday of February for the President, Vice-President, and Legislative Assembly; second Sunday of February for municipal elections.[[16]](#cite_note-16) | Presidential republic
Europe | Sunday.[[17]](#cite_note-Schakel-17) | Parliamentary republic
Europe | Elections for the [Folketing](/wiki/Folketing "Folketing") usually occur on Tuesday, but this is not a statutory requirement.[[18]](#cite_note-Denmark-18) | Parliamentary monarchy
South America | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Presidential republic
North America | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Presidential republic

Europe | Elections for the [Riigikogu](/wiki/Riigikogu "Riigikogu"), which chooses both the president and prime minister, are on the first Sunday of March.[[19]](#cite_note-19) | Parliamentary republic
Europe | Sunday.[[20]](#cite_note-20) | Parliamentary republic
Europe | Sunday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Semi-presidential republic
Europe | Sunday. Elections to the Bundestag may be held on another public holiday.[[21]](#cite_note-21) | Federal parliamentary republic
Europe | Sunday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary republic
Asia | Sunday. |
Europe | Sunday.[[22]](#cite_note-22) | Parliamentary republic
Europe | Saturday.[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Parliamentary republic

Asia | Elections are held over multiple days.[[23]](#cite_note-Massicotte-23) The [2019 Indian general election](/wiki/2019_Indian_general_election "2019 Indian general election"), which began on a Thursday, took place over seven phases with six days between each phase. Constituencies vote only on the day of their respective phase.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25) | Federal parliamentary republic

Asia | Elections are typically held on a Wednesday since [2009](/wiki/2009_Indonesian_presidential_election "2009 Indonesian presidential election") for presidential elections, and since [2014](/wiki/2014_Indonesian_legislative_election "2014 Indonesian legislative election") for legislative elections; both elections have been held on the same day since [2019](/wiki/2019_Indonesian_general_election "2019 Indonesian general election"). Election dates are determined by the General Elections Commission (Indonesian: *Komisi Pemilihan Umum*, KPU), but must occur "on a holiday or a day determined as a national holiday" per the [Indonesian electoral law of 2017](/wiki/Indonesian_electoral_law_of_2017 "Indonesian electoral law of 2017").[[26]](#cite_note-26) | Presidential republic
Asia | Friday for presidential elections.[[27]](#cite_note-27) |

Europe | Typically on a Friday, but precise date set by [Minister for Housing, Planning and Local Government](/wiki/Minister_for_Housing,_Planning_and_Local_Government "Minister for Housing, Planning and Local Government").[[28]](#cite_note-Ireland-28) | Parliamentary republic

Asia | By law on the third Tuesday of [Cheshvan](/wiki/Cheshvan "Cheshvan"), but normally held on a different day.[[29]](#cite_note-Israel-29) Election day is a holiday in Israel, so people do not have to work.[[30]](#cite_note-30) | Parliamentary republic

Europe | Municipal, provincial, and regional elections take place on a Sunday, as do elections for the [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_(Italy) "Chamber of Deputies (Italy)"), the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_of_the_Republic_(Italy) "Senate of the Republic (Italy)") and EU Parliament elections. Occasionally Mondays are added as voting day.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary republic
Asia | Sunday.[[31]](#cite_note-31) | Parliamentary monarchy
Europe | Saturday.[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Parliamentary republic

Asia | Until 2009, elections were held over several consecutive Sundays.[[32]](#cite_note-32) The most recent election, the [2018 Lebanese general election](/wiki/2018_Lebanese_general_election "2018 Lebanese general election"), was also held on a Sunday. | Parliamentary republic

Europe | Elections for the [Seimas](/wiki/Seimas "Seimas") are on the second Sunday of October, and for the president the last Sunday two months before the end of the current president's term.[[33]](#cite_note-33) | Parliamentary republic
Eu

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Election+day%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Election+day%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Election+day%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Election+day%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

or **Polling Day** is the day on which [general elections](/wiki/General_election "General election") are held. In many countries, general elections are always held on a Saturday or Sunday, to enable as many [voters](/wiki/Voter "Voter") as possible to participate; while in other countries elections are always held on a [weekday](/wiki/Week-day_names "Week-day names"). However, some countries, or regions within a country, which hold elections on a weekday declare election day a [public holiday](/wiki/Public_holiday "Public holiday"). Countries which permit [absentee ballots](/wiki/Absentee_ballots "Absentee ballots"), early ballots or [postal votes](/wiki/Postal_vote "Postal vote") to be cast by mail before the election avoid the problem altogether by enabling voters to vote on a day that is more convenient to them.
when the results of the election are tallied and winners are announced.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Election Day (politics)](/w/index.php?title=Election_Day_(politics)&redirect=no "Election Day (politics)"))

Day when elections are held

This article is about the general term "election day" worldwide. For other uses, see [Election day (disambiguation)](/wiki/Election_day_(disambiguation) "Election day (disambiguation)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Election_day "Special:EditPage/Election day") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Election day"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Election+day%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Election+day%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Election+day%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Election+day%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Election+day%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Election+day%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(November 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Election_day.svg/500px-Election_day.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Election_day.svg)

Election day by weekday[*[needs update](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*]

Monday

several days

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4f/SF_City_Hall_Election_Day_2018.jpg/250px-SF_City_Hall_Election_Day_2018.jpg)](/wiki/File:SF_City_Hall_Election_Day_2018.jpg)

[San Francisco City Hall](/wiki/San_Francisco_City_Hall "San Francisco City Hall") illuminated in special LED lighting with the national colors of red, white, and blue on [Election Day in the United States](/wiki/Election_Day_(United_States) "Election Day (United States)") (Tuesday 7 November 2018) to commemorate the occasion

**Election Day** or **Polling Day** is the day on which [general elections](/wiki/General_election "General election") are held. In many countries, general elections are always held on a Saturday or Sunday, to enable as many [voters](/wiki/Voter "Voter") as possible to participate; while in other countries elections are always held on a [weekday](/wiki/Week-day_names "Week-day names"). However, some countries, or regions within a country, which hold elections on a weekday declare election day a [public holiday](/wiki/Public_holiday "Public holiday"). Countries which permit [absentee ballots](/wiki/Absentee_ballots "Absentee ballots"), early ballots or [postal votes](/wiki/Postal_vote "Postal vote") to be cast by mail before the election avoid the problem altogether by enabling voters to vote on a day that is more convenient to them.

Sundays are the most common day for elections, but this is less true in the [Anglosphere](/wiki/Anglosphere "Anglosphere"); Saturdays are used in New Zealand and Australia, and weekdays for the United States, United Kingdom, and Canada. This is partially due to the influence of Protestantism, which historically set restrictions on activities other than church-going during the [Sabbath](/wiki/Sabbath "Sabbath") (usually considered as falling on a Sunday).[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1)

An election day usually culminates in an **election night** when the results of the election are tallied and winners are announced.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

| [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina") | South America | Fourth Sunday of October immediately before the end of the current mandates.[[4]](#cite_note-4) | federal presidential republic |

Election dates are determined by the General Elections Commission (Indonesian: *Komisi Pemilihan Umum*, KPU), but must occur "on a holiday or a day determined as a national holiday" per the [Indonesian electoral law of 2017](/wiki/Indonesian_electoral_law_of_2017 "Indonesian electoral law of 2017").[[26]](#cite_note-26) | Presidential republic |
| [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran") | Asia | Friday for presidential elections.[[27]](#cite_note-27) |  |
| [Ireland](/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland") | Europe | Typically on a Friday, but precise date set by [Minister for Housing, Planning and Local Government](/wiki/Minister_for_Housing,_Planning_and_Local_Government "Minister for Housing, Planning and Local Government").[[28]](#cite_note-Ireland-28) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") | Asia | By law on the third Tuesday of [Cheshvan](/wiki/Cheshvan "Cheshvan"), but normally held on a different day.[[29]](#cite_note-Israel-29) Election day is a holiday in Israel, so people do not have to work.[[30]](#cite_note-30) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") | Europe | Municipal, provincial, and regional elections take place on a Sunday, as do elections for the [Chamber of Deputies](/wiki/Chamber_of_Deputies_(Italy) "Chamber of Deputies (Italy)"), the [Senate](/wiki/Senate_of_the_Republic_(Italy) "Senate of the Republic (Italy)") and EU Parliament elections. Occasionally Mondays are added as voting day.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan") | Asia | Sunday.[[31]](#cite_note-31) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia") | Europe | Saturday.[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon") | Asia | Until 2009, elections were held over several consecutive Sundays.[[32]](#cite_note-32) The most recent election, the [2018 Lebanese general election](/wiki/2018_Lebanese_general_election "2018 Lebanese general election"), was also held on a Sunday. | Parliamentary republic |
| [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania") | Europe | Elections for the [Seimas](/wiki/Seimas "Seimas") are on the second Sunday of October, and for the president the last Sunday two months before the end of the current president's term.[[33]](#cite_note-33) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg") | Europe | Sunday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Macau](/wiki/Macau "Macau") | Asia | Sunday.[[34]](#cite_note-34) |  |
| [Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia") | Europe | Sunday, but the [2020 North Macedonian parliamentary election](/wiki/2020_North_Macedonian_parliamentary_election "2020 North Macedonian parliamentary election") departed from this standard. It was held over three weekdays after being postponed due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[35]](#cite_note-35) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Malta](/wiki/Malta "Malta") | Europe | Saturday.[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") | Asia | Saturday | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") | North America | First Sunday of July until 2018. From 2021, the first Sunday of June.[[36]](#cite_note-36) | Federal presidential republic |
| [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro") | Europe | Sunday for Parliamentary elections.[[37]](#cite_note-37) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") | Europe | Typically on a Wednesday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) For elections to the [European Parliament](/wiki/European_Parliament "European Parliament"), Thursday.[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") | Oceania | Saturday.[[39]](#cite_note-nzhistory_election_days-39) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Nicaragua](/wiki/Nicaragua "Nicaragua") | North America | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Presidential republic |
| [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") | Europe | Monday in early September. Exact date set by the [King of Norway](/wiki/King_of_Norway "King of Norway").[[40]](#cite_note-NorwayElectionDay-40) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Panama](/wiki/Panama "Panama") | North America | Sunday.[[41]](#cite_note-41) | Presidential republic |
| [Paraguay](/wiki/Paraguay "Paraguay") | South America | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Presidential republic |
| [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru") | South America | Sunday.[[42]](#cite_note-42) | Presidential republic |
| [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") | Asia | Second Monday in May.[[43]](#cite_note-Lazo-43) | Presidential republic |
| [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") | Europe | Sunday.[[44]](#cite_note-44) | Semi-presidential republic |
| [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal") | Europe | Sunday.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Puerto Rico](/wiki/Puerto_Rico "Puerto Rico") | North America | Day after the first Monday in November.[[45]](#cite_note-45) |  |
| [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania") | Europe | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Parliamentary republic |
| [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") | Europe | Sunday.[[46]](#cite_note-cikrf-46) | Presidential republic |
| [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia") | Europe | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Semi-presidential republic |
| [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") | Asia | Saturday. When Lee Hsien Loong was a PM, election day was on Friday. By law, polling day is a public holiday if it falls on a weekday.[[47]](#cite_note-47) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia") | Europe | Saturday.[[48]](#cite_note-48) | Parliamentary republic |
| [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") | Europe | Sunday.[[14]](#cite_note-Oireachtas-14) | Parliamentary republic |
| [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea") | Asia | Usually Wednesday (although the most recent election in [2025](/wiki/2025_South_Korean_presidential_election "2025 South Korean presidential election") was held on a Tuesday). Election day is a national holiday.[[49]](#cite_note-SKorea-49) | Presidential republic |
| [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") | Europe | There is no fixed election day for general elections, although since 1986 every general election has been held on Sunday.[[n. 1]](#cite_note-51) Municipal and provincial elections take place on the fourth Sunday of May.[[5]](#cite_note-Electoral_Reform-5)[[51]](#cite_note-52) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") | Europe | Second Sunday of September.[[52]](#cite_note-53)[[53]](#cite_note-54) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland") | Europe | Saturday and Sunday.[[54]](#cite_note-MoJ-55) | Federal parliamentary republic |
| [Taiwan](/wiki/Taiwan "Taiwan") | Asia | Saturday.[[55]](#cite_note-Taiwan-56)[[1]](#cite_note-Brett-1) | Semi-presidential republic |
| [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") | Asia | Sunday.[[56]](#cite_note-57) | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey") | Europe | Sunday.[[57]](#cite_note-58) | Presidential republic |
| [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") | Europe | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Semi-presidential republic |
| [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") | Europe | Thursday.[[54]](#cite_note-MoJ-55) See also: [Election Day (United Kingdom)](/wiki/Election_Day_(United_Kingdom) "Election Day (United Kingdom)") | Parliamentary monarchy |
| [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") | North America | All federal elections take place the Tuesday after the first Monday in November.[[58]](#cite_note-US-59) See also: [Election Day (United States)](/wiki/Election_Day_(United_States) "Election Day (United States)") | Federal presidential republic |
| [Uruguay](/wiki/Uruguay "Uruguay") | South America | Sunday.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] | Presidential republic |
| [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") | South America | Sunday.[[59]](#cite_note-60) | Presidential republic |
| [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam") | Asia | Sunday.[[60]](#cite_note-61) |  |

## Other bodies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Election_day&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Other bodies")]

[Elections to the European Parliament](/wiki/Elections_to_the_European_Parliament "Elections to the European Parliament") take place over a period of four days (i.e., Thursday through to Sunday), according to the election days of the EU members states (as listed above). There are some exceptions; as Wednesday was not covered by the available dates, the Netherlands holds elections on Thursday, while Denmark holds elections on Sunday. Countries that hold the ballot before Sunday are not permitted to announce results until all other countries have finished voting.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

"Accordingly, all Vietnamese citizens satisfying the law-specified conditions are entitled to exercise the rights to vote and to stand for election; the Vietnamese State has the obligation to legally guarantee and create favorable conditions for citizens to exercise their rights. The current election law makes it clearer by stipulating that elections will be held on Sundays so that voters may arrange their schedules to go to the polls while polling places must be located at areas convenient for voters and those who cannot go to polling booths may cast their votes with mobile ballot boxes."
